testcases.input: deck = [17,13,11,2,3,5,7] Output: [2,13,3,11,5,17,7] Explanation: We get the deck in the order [17,13,11,2,3,5,7] (this order does not matter), and reorder it. After reordering, the deck starts as [2,13,3,11,5,17,7], where 2 is the top of the deck. We reveal 2, and move 13 to the bottom. The deck is now [3,11,5,17,7,13]. We reveal 3, and move 11 to the bottom. The deck is now [5,17,7,13,11]. We reveal 5, and move 17 to the bottom. The deck is now [7,13,11,17]. We reveal 7, and move 13 to the bottom. The deck is now [11,17,13]. We reveal 11, and move 17 to the bottom. The deck is now [13,17]. We reveal 13, and move 17 to the bottom. The deck is now [17]. We reveal 17. Since all the cards revealed are in increasing order, the answer is correct.
